calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Continued To Mar-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Denying Defendant Chipotle Services, Llc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Nima Darouian of Messner Reeves LLP, Tel. No. 1-310-909-7440 appearing for the
Defendant;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Chipotle Services, LLC's motion for reconsideration of the June 6, 2018 order denying its petition to compel arbitration is granted and, after reconsideration, plaintiff Rebecca Camping is required to arbitrate all of her claims except her PAGA claim and this case, including the non-arbitrable PAGA claim, is stayed pending the completion of arbitration proceedings. The declaration of Hadley Brink, which relies on statements made by Ms. Camping at the May 14, 2018 hearing, provides new facts to warrant reconsideration of the earlier order. In light of this new evidence, Chipotle has satisfied its burden of showing that Ms. Camping electronically signed the arbitration agreement. While the arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable, Ms. Camping has not shown that the agreement is substantively unconscionable. The failure to attach the JAMS rules is a minor issue. The agreement does not have an unconscionable fee shifting provision. Paragraph 7 of the agreement provides that Ms. Camping is not required to pay any cost or expense that she would not be required to pay if her claims were adjudicated in court. Paragraph 8 refers to the arbitrator's discretion to award fees, but that discretion is "subject to applicable law." The carve-out for injunctive relief is not unconscionable. Paragraph 8, which provides that the parties can go to court for provisional remedies, repeats the right to provisional remedies that is in CCP 1281.8. The arbitration obligations of the agreement are bilateral. There is no carve-out for employer type claims to be adjudicated in court. (See Fitz v. NCR Corp. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 702, 725.) The agreement does not limit Ms. Camping's ability to conduct discovery. Ms. Camping mischaracterizes the JAMS rule, which provides that each party may take at least one deposition. The court severs and stays prosecution of Ms. Camping's PAGA claim. (Iskanian v. CLS Transp. Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348, 384.) Order signed. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Continued To Sep-12-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances :
Atty. Matthew J. Wayne of the Armstrong Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-331-4400 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Rebecca Camping, plaintiff also present in court;
Atty. Nima Darouian of the Messner Reeves LLP, Tel. No. 1-310-909-7440 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - Ms. Rebecca Camping was sworn and examined. The matter was argued. The court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT CHIPOTLE SERVICES, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings is Denied. Chipotle does not prove the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by a preponderance of the evidence. The court gives credit to Ms. Camping of denying or ever reviewing or signing an arbitration agreement. The Brink declaration does not sufficiently establish that Camping in fact did sign an arbitration agreement. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
